The plaintiff requests that the Utterback case be consolidated as well because the cancer allegation is being dropped from the complaint. After hearing argument, the court takes the matter under submission.

Pertinent pre-hardship motions in limine for the cases are discussed by court and counsel. The court will decide whether or not to consolidate Utterback on Tuesday morning (10/11/11). Jury hardshipping will be conducted that day in the afternoon.

The court takes a recess from 3:11 p.m. to 3:15 p.m.

The court will need a generic statement of the case from counsel on Tuesday morning. Counsel will not need to be present for jury hardshipping. The motion to consolidate Utterback will be served on defendants in Groups 1 and 2 as well as the Utterback defense counsel.

The cases will reconvene on Tuesday, October 11, 2011 at 10:00 a.m. for pre-hardship motions in limine, the consolidation ruling on Utterback and the generic statement of the case. Jury hardshipping will commence on that day at 1:30 p.m. Voir dire will take place on Wednesday, 10/12/11, at 9:30 a.m.

At 3:30 p.m., this matter is adjourned.

RULING:
DEFENDANT NACCO MATERIALS HANDLING GROUP, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant NACCO Materials Handling Group, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to negligence and strict liability, plaintiff's deposition testimony and Mr. Libby's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. As to false representation and punitive damages, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting those causes of action. Defendant's separate statement is not demonstrate that defendant propounded contention discovery seeking all facts, witnesses and documents supporting those claims or that plaintiff's responses thereto were factually devoid. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Christopher D. Strunk, Esq., of GORDON & REES (415-986-5900), appearing for defendant / moving party Air & Liquid Systems Corp., Successor by Merger, to Buffalo Pumps, Inc.
Jennifer Alesio, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Andrew Thompson

RULING:
DEFENDANT AIR & LIQUID SYSTEMS CORP. SUCCESSOR BY MERGER, TO BUFFALO PUMPS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Air & Liquid Systems Corp., successor by merger to Buffalo Pumps, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 (negligence, strict liability and sophisticated user) and taken off calendar as to issues 5 and 6 (false representation and punitive damages. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Further, defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that the packing and/or gaskets he removed from Buffalo pumps were original and that plaintiff knew of the hazards of asbestos when he performed the work at issue. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Moreover, proof that a plaintiff has knowledge of the safety hazards of asbestos is not, in and of itself, sufficient to successfully invoke the sophisticated user defense, at least where, as here, there is insufficient evidence that the plaintiff knew that defendant's product contained asbestos. Plaintiff represented that he dismissed false representation and punitive damages, although the court does not see that a dismissal has been filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
DEFENDANT ARMSTRONG INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Armstrong International, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing materials attributable to defendant. By failing to submit plaintiff's responses to defendant's contention discovery with its moving papers, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Citing to and attaching plaintiff's responses to General Order No. 129 standard interrogatories is insufficient. Order signed in open court.
